title: Toroidal Wrapping, Object Bounds, Viewports, Resize, and Scaling · Program Brief: Toroidal Wrap
summary: Objects wrap when centers cross, disappear at seams, collide incorrectly across edges, resize teleports the world, high-DPI scaling distorts input, and zero-sized windows divide by zero. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the toroidal wrap increment in seam-safe logical viewport and resize policy. Learners progress through prediction, explici…
objectives: Wrap complete object bounds with modular arithmetic and test overshoot across corners and large time steps.
Choose center, radius, Rect, FRect, and visual bounds deliberately and keep float world state separate from raster bounds.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Asteroids team handles case 4394 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to wrap full object bounds across seams while preserving logical world state through resize and display scaling; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. A defensible solution applies Toroidal Wrap, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to seam-safe logical viewport and resize policy.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-wrap-viewport-resize-4
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-wrap-viewport-resize-4.
# Case 1655: a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling.
# Operating constraint: test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases.
# Changed case: inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death.
def worked_asteroids_wrap_viewport_resize_4(x: float = -4.0, y: float = 605.0, width: float = 800.0, height: float = 600.0, radius: float = 12.0, resize_preserves_world: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "playerworked4",
        "seed1655",
        "input4dekrow",
        "frameworked41655",
        "state1655worked4",
        "playtest4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if width <= 0.0 or height <= 0.0 or radius < 0.0:
        return False, width, "viewport-domain"
    wrapped_x = ((x + radius) % (width + 2 * radius)) - radius
    wrapped_y = ((y + radius) % (height + 2 * radius)) - radius
    if not resize_preserves_world:
        return False, wrapped_x, "resize-teleport"
    if not (-radius <= wrapped_x < width + radius):
        return False, wrapped_x, "wrap-x"
    assert -radius <= wrapped_y < height + radius
    return True, round(wrapped_x, 3), "seam-safe-wrap"

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-toroidal-wrap-1jnwpe
# Competency: asteroids-toroidal-wrap.
# Case 5154: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def evidence_asteroids_toroidal_wrap_1jnwpe(x: float = -4.0, y: float = 605.0, width: float = 800.0, height: float = 600.0, radius: float = 12.0, resize_preserves_world: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1jnwpe = "1jnwpe-epwnj1-5154"
    assert evidence_variant_1jnwpe
    evidence_axes_1jnwpe = (
        "player1jnwpe",
        "seed5154",
        "inputepwnj1",
        "frame1jnwpe5154",
        "state51541jnwpe",
        "playtestepwnj15154",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1jnwpe)) == 6
    if width <= 0.0 or height <= 0.0 or radius < 0.0:
        return False, width, "viewport-domain"
    wrapped_x = ((x + radius) % (width + 2 * radius)) - radius
    wrapped_y = ((y + radius) % (height + 2 * radius)) - radius
    if not resize_preserves_world:
        return False, wrapped_x, "resize-teleport"
    if not (-radius <= wrapped_x < width + radius):
        return False, wrapped_x, "wrap-x"
    assert -radius <= wrapped_y < height + radius
    return True, round(wrapped_x, 3), "seam-safe-wrap"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Toroidal Wrap model · Theory Toroidal Wrap
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Toroidal Wrap: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 7632 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to wrap full object bounds across seams while preserving logical world state through resize and display scaling; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Wrap complete object bounds with modular arithmetic and test overshoot across corners and large time steps.
